threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look puzzled when the bus driver shouts 'next stop' and the whole bus mutters back, like some strange ritual.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded everyone with his guitar. Surprisingly decent, but the best bit was the old lady who kept telling him to play 'Wonderwall'.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a massive street mural of a cat wearing sunglasses and realized the neighborhood vibe is unapologetically quirky.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find spontaneous raves in abandoned buildings where strangers become friends over shared beats and brews.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by their casual dismissal of the newest burrito joint’s hype; real ones still rave about the hole-in-the-wall on 24th.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just witnessed a guy serenading a entire bus with a ukulele at 5th and Market. The driver smiled, but the passengers were mostly confused.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often don’t realize that the best vermut in Gràcia is found in unmarked places, not the trendy bars on the main streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still taking selfies with the giant mosaic lizard; locals know it’s just a photo op, not a destination.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stop at every traffic light, instead of just sailing through when the coast is clear. It’s a rookie mistake, mate.
Last night on the 35, some bloke serenaded us with 90s boy band hits. You never know what you'll get after midnight in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I realized I lived here was the day I saw an impromptu open-air library springing up in a park, books just left on a bench.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find a spontaneous dance party in the U-Bahn at 2 AM, complete with a DJ and a crowd of locals. Only in Kreuzberg!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they order an egg and chorizo burrito without asking for extra salsa—it's all about the perfect ratio.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a bus where a toddler was leading an impromptu sing-along. Even the driver cracked a smile, but the rest of us were just stuck in traffic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is all about Gaudí; many miss the local murals that tell real stories of the neighborhood's past.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their bewilderment at how many people will squeeze onto a single bus during rush hour. It’s like a sardine can!
